methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $2M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Orbital Composites is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Cole Nielsen that has raised $6.6M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of nine private robotics firms and one public comparable (277810.KQ), though narrow peer filtering—same-stage, adjacent-stage, similar-tier—yielded insufficient datapoints for reliable median-based triangulation.

The stage-progression method is the primary applicable approach. Using total capital raised ($6.6M) as a proxy base value anchored at $2M, a standard seed-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 2.5x yields an implied valuation of $6M. This method captures typical venture progression cadence in the robotics sector without relying on sparse peer multiples. The peer-median approach was not applicable; while 11 same-sector peers exist (including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics), none met the narrow filtering criteria, and an all-sector median would introduce unjustified noise given stage and tier heterogeneity.

The triangulated range is $5M to $7M, with a median of $6M. This reflects reliance on a single method and carries material uncertainty around comparable growth trajectory and market timing assumptions embedded in the 2.5x multiplier. Key risks to valuation include differentiation within an increasingly populated robotics space and capital efficiency relative to raised capital. A narrower range would require either stronger same-stage peer data or evidence of substantive commercial traction.
